name: Clean-State Preflight Protocol (PR-F2, handover F-003)

design_notes.nature_of_this_feature: Handover F-003. Author ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md: the reusable clean-state preflight protocol and its machine-readable verdict convention. Per .agent/feature_list_005.json feature 033 and product_spec_005.md FR-02, the protocol MUST state exactly one of three verdicts (PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL, per docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md Sec 'Preflight Verdict'), explicit blocking rules, the explicit operator-exception rule (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS requires structured operator approval before execution continues -- no train starts from FAIL), and the machine-readable verdict artifact convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json` (per the NIP's 'Artifact Locations' section and feature 043's required first-run output). This feature is protocol-authoring only: no schema (038 owns schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json), no tooling (041 owns the CLI), no capability routing or module-index prose (032 owns ecosystem/README.md, 040 owns NIZAM.json/tools/skill.json/tools/SKILL.md).
design_notes.planned_document_structure: ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md will use numbered '## N. Title' sections, matching the house pattern in methodology/*.md (e.g. methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md): '## 1. Overview', '## 2. When to Run', '## 3. Verdict' (states all three backtick-wrapped verdicts -- `PASS`, `PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS`, `FAIL` -- and the literal phrase 'exactly one'), '## 4. Blocking Rules' (names concrete FAIL-triggering conditions and the literal phrase 'MUST NOT proceed'), '## 5. Operator-Exception Rule' (states `PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS`, the phrase 'operator approval', and the literal phrase 'before execution continues', mirroring the NIP's own wording), '## 6. Verdict Artifact' (states the literal path convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json`), '## 7. References' (points back to the NIP and, forward, to schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json once 038 lands -- as a prose cross-reference only, not a path this feature indexes). Frontmatter: id 'nizam-ecosystem-clean-state-preflight', status 'active' (matching every other shipped protocol/module doc's house pattern -- draft/active/deprecated has no separate 'proposed' value), version '0.1.0' (first cut, matching ecosystem/README.md's own 0.1.0), authoritative_source 'ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md' (self-referential, matching the README.md and NIP-0001 house pattern). This structure is committed to here because the anchored, section-scoped verification entries below (5-8) reference these exact heading strings; the generator's implementation step MUST use them verbatim or the verification commands will legitimately fail (not a scope violation -- a heading-text mismatch is a defect to fix, per Circuit Breaker attempt 1: 'read the exact error, fix the specific line').
design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof: Before proposing the anchored checks below, the section-scoped primitive (tools/verify_lib.sh vlib_section_grep, the same anti-vacuity-approved primitive contract 031 rev1 used to fix its whole-file-grep false-pass) was dry-run against two throwaway fixtures built in the scratchpad (never committed, no repo-tree side effect): a GOOD fixture matching the planned structure verbatim, and a BAD fixture that keeps the three verdict headings/tokens but omits the 'MUST NOT proceed' blocking phrase and the artifact-path line. Result: all 10 positive assertions (three verdict tokens + 'exactly one' in Sec 3; FAIL-token + 'MUST NOT proceed' in Sec 4; PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS-token + 'operator approval' + 'before execution continues' in Sec 5; the literal artifact path in Sec 6) returned exit 0 against GOOD; the two BAD-fixture negative probes (missing 'MUST NOT proceed', missing the artifact path) and a missing-heading probe all returned exit 1, as required. This proves the checking mechanism itself is sound and non-vacuous (rejects a plausible-looking but incomplete doc) independent of, and prior to, the real document being authored -- the real document does not yet exist in the working tree (confirmed absent below), so this dry run against disposable fixtures was the only way to prove the mechanism ahead of authoring, matching the spirit of contract 031's rev1 fixture-probe precedent. [Corrected in rev1 per .agent/qa/033-contract-review.json observation (non-blocking): the anti-vacuity master proof (entry 2) covers the content-anchored checks entries 3-8 specifically, not '3-9' -- entry 9 (validate.sh green) is a repo-wide regression check, not a content-anchored check keyed to the new file's existence.]
design_notes.concurrency_fence: Feature 032 (Ecosystem Module Index) is being implemented CONCURRENTLY in this same working tree by another agent, with contracted paths ecosystem/README.md, NIZAM.json, .agent/contracts/032.json, .agent/evidence/032*, .agent/qa/032*. This feature's scope-guard (verification entry 11) allow-lists ecosystem/README.md and NIZAM.json SOLELY to tolerate 032's concurrent legitimate edits without a false-positive scope-guard failure -- exactly as contract 031 allow-listed the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md it did not touch. This feature's own files_create/files_modify below deliberately OMIT both paths: this feature does not create, edit, or delete ecosystem/README.md or NIZAM.json under any circumstance. Because 032 may legitimately change those two files during the same window, a blind 'byte-identical to HEAD' non-goal check (the pattern contract 031 used for NIZAM.json/schema/tools/ecosystem, verification entry ~13) is NOT applicable to them here without producing a false failure against 032's own legitimate work; the boundary is instead enforced structurally (they are absent from this contract's authorized scope, named explicitly in non_goals, and Validator Mode B reviews this feature's own diff/authorship for the two paths, not a whole-repo diff). The non-goal check for schema/ and tools/ (verification entry 13) remains a plain byte-identical-to-HEAD assertion because no other in-flight feature in this dispatch touches those directories.
design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts: This is a CONTRACT-PROPOSAL step (status=proposed): no implementation edits, no git commit, and no .agent/run_state.json rewrite happen under this document -- run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally per the dispatch instructions. Verification entry 11 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) [corrected in rev1 per .agent/qa/033-contract-review.json observation, non-blocking: this prose previously said 'entry 12', an off-by-one against the actual array position] mirrors the precedent in contracts 025/028/031 exactly: it excludes only this feature's own new 033.json contract and 033*.json qa/review artifacts, and deliberately does NOT check .agent/run_state.json or prior feature_list_00N.json (the orchestrator's own routinely-and-legitimately-modified concern, not a per-feature acceptance test).
design_notes.rev1_summary: REVISION 1 of 3 (per .agent/qa/033-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED). One blocking finding (HIGH, verification_index 14), fixed here; nothing else in scope/non_goals/estimated_lines changed. FIX: entry 14's original `! ls <8 forbidden filenames> >/dev/null 2>&1` false-passed because GNU ls's multi-argument exit code reflects 'was ANY argument missing', not 'were ALL missing' -- the evaluator reproduced this live by creating exactly one forbidden file (ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md) and showing the check still printed PASS. Replaced with a per-file `for`/`[ -e ]` loop that fails as soon as ANY one of the 8 named files exists. Re-proven in this revision with the evaluator's own single-document-creation probe: created ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md, confirmed the revised entry 14 now correctly FAILs ('out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md'), removed the probe, confirmed the revised entry 14 returns to PASS, and confirmed `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- ecosystem/` is empty afterward (no residue). Two harmless, non-blocking prose cross-reference imprecisions the evaluator also flagged (design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts saying 'entry 12' instead of 'entry 11'; design_notes.verification_mechanism_dry_run_proof saying 'entries 3-9' instead of the more precise 'entries 3-8') were also corrected in this revision for cleanliness, though neither blocked approval on its own.

files_create.purpose: The reusable clean-state preflight protocol: exactly one of three verdicts (PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL), explicit blocking rules, the explicit operator-exception rule (PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS requires structured operator approval before execution continues), and the machine-readable verdict artifact convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json`. UDS frontmatter per the house pattern (see design_notes.planned_document_structure).

non_goals: No edit to ecosystem/README.md -- module navigation/lifecycle-index prose is feature 032's scope, not this feature's, even though 032 is concurrently in flight in this same tree (see design_notes.concurrency_fence); this feature's scope-guard tolerates 032's concurrent edits to that path without authorizing this feature to make any of its own
No edit to NIZAM.json -- capability/module indexing is features 032 and 040's scope; same concurrency-tolerance-without-authorization reasoning as above

No creation or edit of schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json or any other schema/*.json file -- the machine-readable schema for the verdict artifact is feature 038's scope; this feature only defines the protocol prose and the artifact PATH convention, not its JSON Schema
No creation or edit of tools/ecosystem_preflight.py or any other file under tools/ -- deterministic preflight tooling is feature 041's scope
No edit to tools/skill.json or tools/SKILL.md -- capability routing is feature 040's scope

No creation or edit of any other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md, 02_evidence_baseline.md, 03_engineering_audit.md, 04-08) -- those belong to features 034, 035, 036, and successor-phase-deferred protocols per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.3
No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for phase 005 is the release feature's (045, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility per product_spec_005.md Sec 2.1; this feature does not touch it
No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- no planning-artifact or debt changes are authorized by this feature

No edit to .agent/run_state.json, any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 033.json, any .agent/qa/*.json, or any prior feature_list_00N.json -- those are immutable audit records or owned by the orchestrator

No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- it is tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure, not because this feature is authorized to modify it
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- the deliverable is verified in the working tree; committing is a separate, later pipeline action
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false

verification.acceptance_test: F-003/AT: the protocol doc file exists.
verification.command: test -f ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md missing"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-vacuity master proof, mirroring the 031 rev1 fixture-probe precedent): ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md does not exist at HEAD, so every content-anchored check below (entries 3-9) is proven non-vacuous by construction -- nothing at HEAD can satisfy them because the target artifact itself does not yet exist there.
verification.command: ! git show HEAD:ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md already exists at HEAD -- content checks below would be vacuous"; exit 1; }

python3 -c "import json,yaml,jsonschema; d=open('ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; jsonschema.validate(yaml.safe_load(d), json.load(open('schema/frontmatter.schema.json')))" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter invalid"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (house-pattern frontmatter fields): id is 'nizam-ecosystem-clean-state-preflight', status is 'active', and authoritative_source is self-referential.
verification.command: python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=open('ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md',encoding='utf-8').read().split('---')[1]; fm=yaml.safe_load(d); ok=(fm.get('id')=='nizam-ecosystem-clean-state-preflight' and fm.get('status')=='active' and fm.get('authoritative_source')=='ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md'); sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)" && echo PASS || { echo "frontmatter house-pattern fields incorrect"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-003/AT (STRENGTHENED from feature_list_005.json's coarse whole-file check per the anti-vacuity mandate -- section-anchored, not vacuous): the '## 3. Verdict' section names all three backtick-wrapped verdicts and states the literal phrase 'exactly one'.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 3\. Verdict$' '`PASS`' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 3\. Verdict$' '`PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS`' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 3\. Verdict$' '`FAIL`' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 3\. Verdict$' 'exactly one' && echo PASS || { echo "Verdict section missing one of: PASS / PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS / FAIL / 'exactly one'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-003/AT (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 4. Blocking Rules' section names `FAIL` and states the literal phrase 'MUST NOT proceed' -- the explicit blocking rule.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 4\. Blocking Rules$' '`FAIL`' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 4\. Blocking Rules$' 'MUST NOT proceed' && echo PASS || { echo "Blocking Rules section missing FAIL token or the 'MUST NOT proceed' rule"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-003/AT (STRENGTHENED, section-anchored): the '## 5. Operator-Exception Rule' section names `PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS` and states both 'operator approval' and 'before execution continues' -- the explicit operator-exception rule.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 5\. Operator-Exception Rule$' '`PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS`' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 5\. Operator-Exception Rule$' 'operator approval' && vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 5\. Operator-Exception Rule$' 'before execution continues' && echo PASS || { echo "Operator-Exception Rule section missing PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS token, 'operator approval', or 'before execution continues'"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (machine-readable verdict artifact convention, per the NIP's 'Artifact Locations' section and feature 043's required first-run output): the '## 6. Verdict Artifact' section states the literal path convention `.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json`.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_section_grep ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md '^## 6\. Verdict Artifact$' '\.agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight\.json' && echo PASS || { echo "Verdict Artifact section missing the .agent/reconciliation/<execution-id>/preflight.json convention"; exit 1; }
bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validate.sh SUMMARY regressed from 11 passed, 0 failed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (scope guard, untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant per design_notes.concurrency_fence -- ecosystem/README.md and NIZAM.json are 032's concurrently in-flight scope, and v0.6.0-release-notes.md is a pre-existing untracked artifact this feature does not touch): only this feature's own new file, plus the tolerated concurrent/pre-existing paths, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (.agent/ immutability guard, mirroring the precedent in contracts 025/028/031, extended per design_notes.concurrency_fence to tolerate feature 032's concurrently in-flight contract/qa artifacts): no historical (pre-existing, already-approved) .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact was edited -- excludes only this feature's own new 033.json contract and 033*.json qa/review artifacts, AND feature 032's own concurrently-authored 032.json contract and 032*.json qa/review artifacts (032 is a different, independently-scoped in-flight feature, not a historical/immutable one).
verification.command: changed=$(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts ':(exclude).agent/contracts/033.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/033*.json' ':(exclude).agent/contracts/032.json' ':(exclude,glob).agent/qa/032*.json'); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then echo 'immutable audit record(s) touched:'; echo "$changed"; exit 1; fi; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): every schema/*.json file and every file under tools/ are byte-identical to HEAD -- this feature performs zero schema/tooling work.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/ && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope schema/tooling file was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, and docs/planning/DEBT.md are byte-identical to HEAD.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- docs/planning/manifest.json docs/planning/ROADMAP.md docs/planning/DEBT.md && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope planning document was modified"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement, REVISED per .agent/qa/033-contract-review.json required_fix, HIGH: the original single multi-argument `ls` call false-passed because GNU ls's exit code reflects whether ANY argument was missing, not whether ALL were -- reproduced by the evaluator: creating exactly one forbidden file still left `ls` exiting nonzero, so the negated check still printed PASS. Replaced with a per-file existence loop that fails as soon as ANY one of the 8 named files exists): no other ecosystem/*.md protocol document (00/02-08) was created.
verification.command: bad=0; for f in ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md ecosystem/04_dependency_reconciliation.md ecosystem/05_release_train_coordination.md ecosystem/06_simplification_review.md ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md ecosystem/08_ga_gate.md; do if [ -e "$f" ]; then echo "out-of-scope ecosystem protocol document created: $f"; bad=1; fi; done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/033-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/033-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/033-verify-14.txt for the fourteenth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 14 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
